five_year_trend.key_shift: Increased focus on Scientific Research GK

subjects.name: General Intelligence (Reasoning)
subjects.analysis: Identical structure across levels but higher abstraction in Graduation level. Non-verbal reasoning is key for Matriculation posts.

subjects.name: Quantitative Aptitude
subjects.analysis: Matriculation level is 100% Arithmetic. Higher Secondary and Graduation levels include Advanced Maths (Algebra, Geometry, Trig).

subjects.name: General Awareness
subjects.analysis: Broad spectrum covering environment, daily observations, and scientific research. Highly unpredictable.

subjects.name: English Language
subjects.analysis: Tests basic knowledge and comprehension. Cloze test and sentence improvement are the scoring sections.

mentorship_data.title: Level-Wise Strategy
mentorship_data.content: Graduates should apply for Matric and HS posts too. You will have to appear for 3 separate exams, giving you 3 chances at a job with one preparation.

mentorship_data.title: Document Scrutiny Warning
mentorship_data.content: Unlike CGL/CHSL, you must send physical hard copies of your documents to Regional Offices after clearing CBT. 25% rejections happen due to late speed-posts.

mentorship_data.title: Essential Qualification (EQ)
mentorship_data.content: Selection Posts are niche. Ensure your Degree/Experience exactly matches the 'Post Code' requirements before applying to avoid rejection at the final stage.

mentorship_data.title: Salary Snapshot 2026
mentorship_data.content: With the latest DA (58%) and 7th CPC revisions, Level 1 in-hand is ~₹26k, while Level 7 (Graduation) can exceed ₹72k in metro cities.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Can I apply for multiple post codes?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, but you must pay separate fees for each. If they belong to the same level, you give one exam; if different levels, separate exams.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there an interview in Selection Post?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, there is no interview for any Selection Post (Matric, HS, or Graduation).
Generally 18-30 years, but it varies by post code. Check the official PDF for specific relaxations.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the total time for the exam?
frequently_asked_questions.a: You get 60 minutes to attempt 100 questions. Speed is critical as there is no sectional timing.
Only for specific 'Technical' or 'Scientific' posts. General posts like MTS or LDC do not require experience.
